admissions_process.summary: Graduate applicants review the catalog and admissions page, submit the required file, and complete any placement, English, or departmental review steps described in the official catalog and admissions materials.
steps: Review the AUT graduate program information in the 2025-2026 catalogue.
Prepare the graduate application file and required supporting documents.
Complete any required placement or English testing.
Follow the admissions office instructions for review and enrollment.

required_documents.summary: The admissions page and catalogue publish a standard graduate file including transcript, degree proof, CV, recommendation letters, and equivalency documentation when needed.
items: Certified copy of the university degree

language_requirements.summary: Graduate applicants from non-English instruction institutions may be required to take International TOEFL with a minimum score of 550 or another internationally recognized English test.
notes: The admissions page also references placement and English testing.

tuition_model.summary: AUT publishes graduate tuition in the admissions page and catalog as per-credit tuition and related graduate fee rows. Program-level tuition remains null in the inventory because tuition is centralized at university scope.

fee_schedule.summary: Official graduate fee rows published in the 2025-2026 catalogue and admissions page.

items.fee_label: Activities / yearbook / technology / internet / library access fee

scholarships.summary: The catalogue and admissions page reference financial aid categories including discounts, merit, sibling, sport, grants, and scholarships, but no numeric graduate scholarship catalogue was published.

financial_aid.summary: Financial aid is available through the admissions process, but no numeric aid table or graduate-wide award schedule was published.

payment_methods.summary: The reviewed official sources mention tuition and semester-based fees, but do not publish a separate graduate payment-method list.

payment_plans.summary: No graduate installment plan or payment schedule was published in the reviewed official sources.

academic_calendar.summary: The official website links to an academic calendar and the catalog includes calendar/regulatory references, but no separate graduate calendar table was published here.

graduate_regulations.summary: Graduate regulations are described in the catalogue and student manual, but no separately published graduate handbook was identified.

international_students.summary: International students are addressed in the catalogue, including visa guidance and passport-validity notes, but no separate graduate international-student policy page was found.
items: Passport validity of at least 13 months
Visa guidance through the Admissions Office

accreditation.summary: The catalogue states that the MBA is fully accredited by the Ministry of Education and Higher Education, but no broader university-wide graduate accreditation statement was isolated.
